methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $60M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Tenna Systems is a seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Avner Bendheim and Gabriel Bendheim, currently valued at $60M post-money on $13.5M raised. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private entities including Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries operating in the same sector and stage proximity.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, applying a typical 2.5x multiplier from Seed to next-stage advancement, yields $150M based on the $60M last post-money valuation. Peer-median analysis examined three same-sector, adjacent-stage comparables: Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, and Ares Industries at $150M, producing a median implied value of $300M with a range of $150M–$500M.

The two-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $128M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $225M. The current $60M valuation sits below both method outputs, reflecting typical seed-stage positioning relative to Series A peers. Key uncertainties include revenue traction and capital efficiency relative to the three named comparables, stage-advancement timeline assumptions embedded in the multiplier, and whether the three-company peer sample adequately represents the sector's dispersion.
